title: Type Checking and Conversion
instructions: Practice checking types and converting between different data types.
hints: Use try-except to handle conversion errors safely
Use isinstance() with a tuple to check multiple types
type(value).__name__ gives the type name as a string

testCases.description: safe_int_conversion should convert a valid string number to int
testCases.validation: assert safe_int_conversion('42') == 42, 'Should convert string 42 to int 42'

testCases.description: safe_int_conversion should return None for non-numeric input
testCases.validation: assert safe_int_conversion('abc') is None, 'Should return None for non-numeric string'

testCases.description: is_numeric should return True for int and float values
testCases.validation: assert is_numeric(10) == True and is_numeric(3.14) == True, 'Should identify int and float as numeric'

testCases.description: is_numeric should return False for non-numeric types
testCases.validation: assert is_numeric('hello') == False, 'Strings should not be considered numeric'

testCases.description: convert_temperature should convert string, add 32, and return as string
get_type_name should return the correct type name string
testCases.validation: assert get_type_name(42) == 'int' and get_type_name('hi') == 'str' and get_type_name([]) == 'list', 'Should return correct type names'

description: Practice Python type checking and conversion by implementing safe type conversions with error handling, using isinstance() for type checks, converting between strings and numbers, and inspecting type names. These skills are essential for writing robust code that handles diverse input types.
